prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial sequence is represented by two sampled frames.

At the beginning, the image is very dark, with little visible detail beyond a black background and a dim horizontal surface or object near the lower part of the frame. Overlaid archival/source markings are visible: a “MyFootage.com” watermark in the upper left, the number “160172” at lower left, and a timecode line reading “TCR 06:07:07:06” along the bottom.

By the final frame, the commercial has shifted to a product/title-card style image. Large white text appears across the upper and middle area, including the words “NEW REVERE STEREO” and “TAPE CARTRIDGE SYSTEM.” Behind or beneath the text there appears to be a small stack or display of rectangular cartridge-like items. The lower portion prominently shows a large “3M” logo with the word “COMPANY” beneath it, indicating the advertised brand/company. The same source watermark and timecode overlays remain visible, including “MyFootage.com,” “160172,” and a bottom timecode reading “TCR 06:07:58:06.”

No people are visible in the sampled frames, and no dialogue, music, or voice-over can be inferred from the images alone.
